# io.add_mutations { #tslai.io.add_mutations }

```python
tslai.io.add_mutations(ts, rate=1e-08, random_seed=None)
```

Overlay biallelic mutations on a tree sequence so tsinfer has variant data.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Tree sequence to mutate (typically a bare, mutationless ARG).

<code>[**rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1e-08]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Per-base, per-generation mutation rate (default ``1e-8``).

<code>[**random_seed**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Seed for the mutation simulation.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``ts`` with biallelic mutations overlaid under ``msprime.BinaryMutationModel``.
